        if max_index != i:

1. Iterate over the array from the end to the first element.
2. For each iteration, find the index of the maximum element in the unsorted part of the array.
3. If the maximum element is not already at its correct position (i.e., it is not at index `i`), flip the array from the start to the index of the maximum element.
4. Then, flip the entire array to move the maximum element to its correct position.

The time complexity of this implementation is O(n^2) in the worst case, where n is the length of the array. This is because in the worst case, the maximum element may be at the end of the array, requiring two flips per iteration.
